decisions.title: Why Context Isolation Matters
decisions.description: A subagent runs with its own conversation history, separate from the parent agent. This prevents the parent's context from being polluted with low-level details of a subtask. Without isolation, a file-editing subtask would inject hundreds of lines of diff content into the parent's context, consuming token budget and confusing future reasoning.
decisions.alternatives: An alternative is to share a single conversation thread and use markers to delineate subtask sections. This is simpler to implement but degrades rapidly: the parent agent cannot distinguish its own reasoning from the subagent's, and context length grows uncontrollably.

zh.title: 为什么上下文隔离很重要
zh.description: 子 Agent 拥有独立的对话历史，与父 Agent 分离。这防止了父级上下文被子任务的低级细节所污染。如果没有隔离，一个文件编辑子任务会向父级上下文注入数百行 diff 内容，消耗 token 预算并干扰后续推理。

decisions.title: Why Share Only Filesystem, Not Memory
decisions.description: Parent and subagent communicate through the filesystem — files written by one can be read by the other. They do not share conversation memory. This mirrors how human teams collaborate: through shared artifacts (documents, code), not by reading each other's minds. It also means the subagent's implementation can change without affecting the parent.
decisions.alternatives: Sharing a memory store (like a vector database) between agents was considered. However, this creates tight coupling and makes it hard to reason about what each agent knows. File-based communication is explicit, debuggable, and naturally persistent.

zh.title: 为什么只共享文件系统而不共享记忆
zh.description: 父 Agent 和子 Agent 通过文件系统通信——一方写入的文件，另一方可以读取，但它们不共享对话记忆。这类似于人类团队的协作方式：通过共享产物（文档、代码）而非读取彼此的思维。这也意味着子 Agent 的实现可以更换，而不影响父 Agent。
